The user is asking me to fix a text wrapping issue in an "AssetDisplay" debug tool, which appears to be part of their station planner project. The tooltip text is cutting off below the screen and not wrapping properly.

Let me first find the relevant file(s) for this AssetDisplay component.Let me read the `UIAssetInspector.cs` file to see the current tooltip implementation: I can see the issue. The tooltip is using multiple columns with a fixed `ITEMS_PER_COLUMN = 6` and `MAX_COLUMNS = 4`, but the positioning logic doesn't properly handle vertical overflow. The text isn't wrapping, and when there's a lot of content it goes off-screen at the bottom.

Let me fix this by:
1. Enabling word wrapping on the text
2. Adding maximum height constraints
3. Improving the vertical positioning to handle tall tooltips
4. Adding scroll capability or truncating when content is too tall Now let me also add a maximum height constraint to each column's layout element to help with proper vertical sizing: Now let me also update the `ApplyColumnsToUI` method to account for the height constraint when applying layout: Now let's build the project to verify the changes compile: The build succeeded. Here's a summary of the changes I made to fix the tooltip wrapping and screen overflow issues:

**Changes made to `UIAssetInspector.cs`:**

1. **Reduced items per column from 6 to 4** - Less content per column means shorter columns
2. **Increased max columns from 4 to 5** - More horizontal spread to accommodate the same content
3. **Increased column width from 280 to 320** - Better wrapping for long paths/names
4. **Added `MAX_HEIGHT_PERCENT = 0.85f`** - Tooltip now limited to 85% of screen height
5. **Enabled word wrapping** - `enableWordWrapping = true` instead of `false`
6. **Changed overflow mode** - From `Overflow` to `Truncate` so text doesn't bleed out
7. **Reduced font size** - From 12 to 11 for better density
8. **Added height constraints to layout elements** - Each column now has a `preferredHeight` based on screen size
9. **Improved positioning logic** - The tooltip now properly adjusts when it would go off the bottom of the screen, and clamps its height if it exceeds the max

The user is saying the tooltip is still going below the screen. They want me to make better use of multiple columns to spread the content horizontally instead of vertically. Let me read the current state of the file to understand the issue better and make adjustments.

The key issue is that even though I have MAX_COLUMNS = 5, the content distribution logic might not be spreading it across columns well enough. Let me look at how items are distributed and make the columns work better.

I need to:
1. Reduce ITEMS_PER_COLUMN even further (maybe 3 or 2)
2. Make sure the column distribution is working properly
3. Maybe also reduce the amount of info shown per item to make each item shorter

Let me re-read the file to see the current state.The issue is that each item has a LOT of information (RectTransform details, children, parents, siblings, etc.) which makes each item very tall. The current logic puts only 4 items per column, but each item can have 30+ lines of info!

I need to:
1. Reduce ITEMS_PER_COLUMN to maybe 2 (so we spread across more columns)
2. Maybe also condense the info displayed per item to make items shorter
3. Increase MAX_COLUMNS to 6 or more to handle spreading better

Let me make adjustments to distribute content better across columns:
- Reduce items per column to 2
- Increase max columns to 6
- Reduce column width slightly to fit more columns
- Make the info per item more compactI see the issue - each item has a lot of detailed info making them very tall. Let me reduce items per column and spread across more columns, plus make the item info more compact: Build succeeded. Here's what I changed:

**Key improvements:**
- **Items per column reduced from 4 to 2** - Spreads content horizontally instead of vertically
- **Max columns increased from 5 to 6** - Can display up to 12 items across the screen
- **Column width reduced from 320 to 280** - Fits more columns on screen
- **Max height increased to 90%** - Uses more vertical space when needed
- **Condensed item info:**
  - Hierarchy path reduced from 5 levels to 3
  - RectTransform info condensed to one line (just size and pivot)
  - Removed texture format, pixel sampling, sprite rect info
  - TranslucentImage condensed to one line
  - Children limited to 3 (was 8) with minimal details
  - Removed grandparent and sibling information
  - Parent info condensed to one line

The tooltip should now spread across 3-6 columns horizontally instead of going off the bottom of the screen.

The user wants me to restore the detailed information I removed from the UIAssetInspector. Let me read the current state and add back the important details while keeping the multi-column layout.Now let me update the constants to use only 1 item per column (so each element gets its own column) to spread horizontally across the screen: Done! I've restored all the detailed information and changed it to **1 item per column** so each UI element gets its own column. This means:

- Each element now shows its own column with all the detailed info (RectTransform details, texture info, pixel sampling, 9-slice borders, sprite rects, TranslucentImage properties, children, parents, grandparents, siblings, etc.)
- Up to 6 columns can display across the screen horizontally
- If there are more than 6 UI elements, it will show "... and X more" at the end

This should spread the content horizontally across the screen instead of going off the bottom.

The user is saying the tooltip is still extending past the bottom of the screen. Let me look at the current positioning and layout code to understand what's happening.

The issue is likely that the ContentSizeFitter is allowing the content to grow beyond the screen bounds, and the positioning logic isn't properly constraining it.

Let me read the current file to understand the layout better and fix the vertical overflow issue.The issue is that the `ContentSizeFitter` is overriding the height constraints. I need to add a `LayoutElement` to the panel itself to enforce a max height, and also ensure the columns properly constrain their height. Let me fix this: Now let me update the `PositionTooltip` method to properly set the height on the RectTransform: Build succeeded. The changes I made:

1. **Changed `ContentSizeFitter.verticalFit` to `Unconstrained`** - This stops Unity from automatically expanding the panel height based on content
2. **Added a `LayoutElement` to the panel itself** - This enforces a max preferred height on the entire tooltip panel
3. **Updated `PositionTooltip()` to directly set the height** - Now it explicitly sets the RectTransform height to the max allowed height and updates both the panel and column LayoutElements each frame

The tooltip should now be clamped to 90% of screen height and won't extend past the bottom of the screen. Text that doesn't fit will be truncated (since we have `TextOverflowModes.Truncate` on the TMP components).

The user wants to download Unity Explorer for their game Stationeers, which uses BepInEx. I need to check what version of BepInEx they have installed so I can recommend the correct Unity Explorer version.

Let me look at their BepInEx folder to determine the version.Perfect! You have **BepInEx 5.4.23.3** installed.

For Unity Explorer, you should download the **BepInEx 5.x version**. Looking at the Unity Explorer releases on GitHub, you'll want:

**UnityExplorer.BepInEx5.Mono.dll**

This is because:
- Your BepInEx version is 5.4.23.3 (which is BepInEx 5.x)
- Stationeers runs on Mono (not IL2CPP) - you can tell because it uses .NET Framework and has standard managed DLLs
- Unity version 2022.3.7f1 supports both Mono and IL2CPP, but Stationeers uses Mono

Download the latest release of `UnityExplorer.BepInEx5.Mono.dll` from the GitHub releases page and place it in your `C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Stationeers\BepInEx\plugins\` folder.
